data.user_prompt: Which Louisville mental health websites have useful service and referral information?
data.system_message: Answer as a local-search assistant for a person in Louisville, Kentucky. Prefer current local providers and cite source URLs when available.
data.tag: lmhg llm response perplexity Which Louisville mental health websites have useful service and referral informa

sections.text:
The most useful Louisville mental health websites for service and referral information are **Mental Health Lou**, **Seven Counties Services**, and **University of Louisville Health (UofL Health)**, as they provide comprehensive directories, crisis support, and community-specific referrals.

| Website | Key Service & Referral Information |
|---------|-----------------------------------|
| **Mental Health Lou** ([mentalhealthlou.com](https://www.mentalhealthlou.com)) | A registered 501(c)(3) non-profit connecting Louisville residents to therapists, wellness events, and school workshops; focuses on reducing stigma and barriers to care in underserved neighborhoods [1]. |
| **Seven Counties Services** ([sevencounties.org](https://sevencounties.org)) | Kentucky’s leading Community Mental Health Center serving Jefferson County and surrounding areas; offers mental health, addiction recovery, and developmental services with specialized content for families and caregivers [3]. |
| **UofL Health – Mental & Behavioral Health** ([uoflhealth.org](https://uoflhealth.org/services/mental-and-behavioral-health/)) | Provides emergency psychiatric services, acute crisis stabilization, substance abuse treatment, and outpatient therapy; includes referral to Peace Hospital for no-charge assessments and lists crisis contact (988) [2]. |
| **Spalding University – Center for Behavioral Health** ([behavioralhealth.spalding.edu](https://behavioralhealth.spalding.edu)) | Offers in-person and virtual counseling and psychological assessments for all ages in downtown Louisville [5]. |
| **Hagan Health** ([haganhealth.com](https://haganhealth.com)) | Provides expert care for anxiety, depression, and ADHD; features a well-respected psychiatrist with 44+ years of experience [4]. |
| **The Brook Hospitals** ([thebrookhospitals.com](https://thebrookhospitals.com)) | Operates two Louisville locations (KMI and Dupont) offering psychiatric and substance abuse treatment for adults 18+ since 1985 [6]. |
| **Norton Healthcare – Behavioral Health** ([nortonhealthcare.com](https://nortonhealthcare.com/services-and-conditions/behavioral-health/)) | Coordinates outpatient mental health care with primary care services across Kentucky and Indiana [8]. |
| **Kentucky Dept. for Behavioral Health** ([dbhdid.ky.gov](https://dbhdid.ky.gov)) | State agency administering funded mental health, substance use, and developmental services; useful for understanding public program eligibility [7]. |

For immediate crisis support, all sites recommend calling **988** (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) or **911** in emergencies. If you need a no-charge assessment, **Peace Hospital** (via UofL Health referral) offers free treatment option assistance [2].

These websites are current as of July 2026 and prioritize local, accessible care in Louisville.
